title: Testing, Static Quality, Performance, and Release · Runtime Trace: Release Evidence
summary: Harden the service-data client with behavioral tests, deterministic fixtures, performance evidence, and a clean release. Planned artifact: Add and verify the js release evidence increment in a tested and release-ready JavaScript application. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, and evidence defense.
objectives: Produce a clean-environment build, supported-runtime record, accessibility checks, rollback plan, and stakeholder acceptance evidence.
Measure startup, long tasks, memory retention, network, rendering, and algorithm cost before optimizing a bottleneck.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

starterFiles.javascript:
// javascript-basics cumulative stakeholder program.

steps.title: Predict Release Evidence
steps.instruction:
theory case — A release candidate passes the familiar 3-record fixture but fails an empty list and a duplicate identifier. Reproduce both, add behavior and accessibility regressions, and reject release until the clean build agrees.

Before reading, predict which action preserves the Release Evidence contract for the supplied data.
steps.why: The decision supplies observable evidence for produce a clean-environment build, supported-runtime record, accessibility checks, rollback plan, and stakeholder acceptance evidence.

content.text: A release candidate passes the familiar 3-record fixture but fails an empty list and a duplicate identifier. Reproduce both, add behavior and accessibility regressions, and reject release until the clean build agrees.

content.title: Commit before feedback
content.text: Choose from the stated data before opening the worked model. Record which Release Evidence condition decided your prediction.

content.text: Produce a clean-environment build, supported-runtime record, accessibility checks, rollback plan, and stakeholder acceptance evidence. Use this as an observable contract: name its preconditions, trace the state or value change, and state what evidence would disprove the result.

content.title: Reasoning move
content.text: Trace concrete runtime values, bindings, receiver, mutation, and queued work in order; assert the observable result, then change one input or event and seek the first causal divergence. Apply that move specifically to Release Evidence while producing a tested and release-ready JavaScript application.

content.title: Worked case pattern
content.text: A release candidate passes the familiar 3-record fixture but fails an empty list and a duplicate identifier. Reproduce both, add behavior and accessibility regressions, and reject release until the clean build agrees. Competency probe: Produce a clean-environment build, supported-runtime record, accessibility checks, rollback plan, and stakeholder acceptance evidence. A defensible solution applies Release Evidence, preserves the governing invariant or value contract, and connects the observed result to a tested and release-ready JavaScript application.

content.caption: Runnable runtime trace example; predict its result before running, then change one boundary input.

content.title: Non-worked case
content.text: A successful local run is sufficient release proof. This fails the Release Evidence contract because it omits a precondition, invariant, ownership rule, or counterexample needed by this exact case.

steps.title: Build Release Evidence
steps.instruction: Add a evidence_js_release_evidence_1s2ees function that derives the changed-case result, asserts one meaningful invariant with console.assert, and returns inspectable evidence.
steps.why: Building a runnable or mechanically reviewable artifact turns Release Evidence into observable performance evidence.

content.text: The Release Evidence structural check is only one gate. a tested and release-ready JavaScript application must also survive the stated changed case and causal explanation.

steps.title: Defend the Release Evidence model
steps.instruction: Write the theory evidence note for Release Evidence: explain the governing condition, one failure, and how this concrete case was verified — A release candidate passes the familiar 4-record fixture but fails an empty list and a duplicate identifier. Reproduce both, add behavior and accessibility regressions, and reject release until the clean build agrees. Competency probe: Produce a clean-environment build, supported-runtime record, accessibility checks, rollback plan, and stakeholder acceptance evidence.
steps.why: Retrieving Release Evidence through the concrete a tested and release-ready JavaScript application case exposes causal gaps that recognition alone can hide.

content.title: Evidence defense
content.text: Use the case, one assumption, one observable result, and one limitation. Do not copy the competency statement: Produce a clean-environment build, supported-runtime record, accessibility checks, rollback plan, and stakeholder acceptance evidence.
